_comment: Validation datasets for RecomFi. 'orthopox_example' uses the bundled
example_data and runs out of the box. The fetch-based datasets are
downloaded by validation/fetch.py from the listed NCBI accessions.
Accessions marked TODO must be confirmed before enabling the dataset
(set enabled=true). See validation/README.md for provenance notes.

datasets.name: orthopox_example
datasets.description: Orthopoxvirus: 8-contig synthetic cowpox query carrying a variola insert.
datasets.provenance: NCBI: camelpox NC_003391, cowpox_KC813504 KC813504.1, cowpox NC_003663, monkeypox NC_063383, taterapox NC_008291, vaccinia NC_006998, variola NC_001611. sibeliaz OOMs on these large collinear blocks on a typical laptop; minimap2 (the assembly-to-reference backend) is the practical choice for this fragmented query.

datasets.name: sarscov2_xbb
datasets.description: SARS-CoV-2 XBB: recombinant of BA.2.10.1 (5') and BA.2.75 (3'), breakpoint in spike.
datasets.provenance: XBB query OQ075387 (NCBI: SARS-CoV-2/human/BGD/icddrb-TND-06-1082/2022, XBB; Bangladesh study PMC10019308). BA.2.75 parent OQ326841 (UT-NCD1757, characterised BA.2.75; PMC10035475). Wuhan-Hu-1 NC_045512.2 (coordinate anchor). BA.2.10.1 parent OR324670 was matched by NCBI text search for 'BA.2.10.1 complete genome' -- confirm its Pango lineage (e.g. via Nextclade) before trusting it. XBB's breakpoint sits in the spike region (~genome nt 22.9 kb).

datasets.name: hiv1_crf
datasets.description: HIV-1 CRF01_AE (CM240) recombinant vs pure subtype reference genomes.
datasets.provenance: All accessions confirmed on NCBI nuccore. Query CRF01_AE = CM240 U54771 (9203 bp). Pure subtype references: B = HXB2 K03455 (coordinate standard, 9719 bp), A1 = 92UG037 U51190, C = U46016, D = ELI K03454. CRF01_AE is a subtype-A-dominant mosaic with an E-like env. With the HMM caller the major parent resolves to A1 and NO recombinant region is called against the pure subtypes -- correctly, because the true env donor (subtype E / CRF01_AE) is absent: the env divergence is reported as a reference-coverage gap (run find-references), not fabricated as a recombination among A1/B/C/D. The old heuristic hallucinated ~6 low-margin regions here.

datasets.name: norovirus_gii
datasets.description: Norovirus GII.P16-GII.1 recombinant: ORF1 (P16 polymerase) vs ORF2/3 (GII.1 capsid).
datasets.provenance: All accessions confirmed on NCBI nuccore. Query MG572182 (Norovirus GII isolate SDJN170450, the GII.P16-GII.1 recombinant; PMC5805878, 7518 bp). Polymerase (ORF1, ~nt 1-5.1 kb) matches a GII.P16 strain -> MK773584 (GII.P16-GII.4 Sydney, 7559 bp); capsid (ORF2/3) matches GII.1 -> U07611 (Hawaii calicivirus, GII.1, 7513 bp). Breakpoint at the ORF1/ORF2 junction (~nt 5.0 kb).
